title: heliotropium racemosum
intro: Heliotropium racemosum, also known as clustered heliotrope, is a perennial plant species within the borage family, characterized by its small, blue or purple flowers that grow in clusters. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Heliotropium racemosum include alleviating coughs, reducing fever, and relieving digestive issues. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional remedies to treat various ailments, including colds, sore throats, and skin conditions.
